# Outcome Based Billing is the Future for AI Products

Vijay Gorfad

Vijay Gorfad

June 15, 2025

7 min read

Outcome Based Billing is the Future for AI Products

The AI industry is undergoing a fundamental transformation in how services are priced and delivered. Traditional subscription models and upfront licensing fees are giving way to a more sophisticated approach: outcome-based billing.

## The Traditional Model's Limitations

### Upfront Investment Barriers

Traditional AI pricing models require significant upfront investments, often before businesses can validate the technology's impact on their operations. This creates a chicken-and-egg problem where companies need to spend money to determine if the AI solution will generate value. [...] ### Focus on Results

When revenue is tied to outcomes, AI companies must prioritize solutions that deliver measurable business value. This naturally leads to better products and services.

### Accelerated Adoption

By removing upfront barriers and guaranteeing results, outcome-based models accelerate AI adoption across industries and company sizes.

## Key Benefits for Businesses

### 1. Reduced Financial Risk

Pay only when you achieve specific, measurable outcomes. No more gambling on unproven technology.

### 2. Faster ROI Realization

Since you're paying for results, positive ROI is built into the model from day one.

### 3. Better Vendor Relationships

Aligned incentives create true partnerships rather than traditional vendor-client relationships.

### 4. Scalable Investment

If you’re running an AI startup, billing is a distraction. Pricing by API calls? GPU usage? Tokens? Many founders I’ve spoken to struggle with figuring out the best model—some even lose money because of unpredictable costs.

We’ve seen AI startups:

Would love to hear how you’re solving this. Have you built an internal system, or are you using a solution? What’s the biggest challenge for you?

For us, we ended up flat fee subscription+ volume of tokens included; and separate billing per preset token number of overload (when included tokens are all consumed).

results.title: AI Pricing Has a Serious Customer Problem
results.content:
the products and services they buy, and corresponds to the value delivered by the AI product. What’s more, as the amount and nature of delivered value evolves, so will customers’ response to AI pricing. [...] #### 4. Outcome-based pricing changes what the customer thinks they are buying.

A recurring proposal in AI pricing commentary is that AI products should be priced based on outcomes that are relevant to customers. Depending on the application, it could be per closed support ticket, per qualified lead, per booked meeting, per merged pull request, per recovered insurance claim, and so on. The straightforward logic behind this idea is that outcome-based pricing shifts the customer’s attention away from tokens and usage units and bases pricing on measures of the economic value delivered by the AI product’s use. See Fin’s outcome-based pricing below.

Fin’s outcome-based pricing, May 2026. [...] what they’re asking for”) and expected to use the product as much as they reasonably wanted after paying the fee.
